---
title: "Assignment 1: Brush up R and Quarto"
author: "Md Al Hasib"
date: "2026-03-07"
format: 
  html:
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
---

## 1. Introduction
This assignment serves as a refresh for R programming and Quarto, utilizing the TEDS2016 dataset to explore political attitudes in Taiwan.

## 2. Data Loading and Preparation
We load the dataset directly from the provided source and handle initial variable labeling.

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| message: false
library(haven)
library(tidyverse)

# Loading the TEDS 2016 dataset
TEDS_2016 <- read_dta("[https://github.com/datageneration/home/blob/master/DataProgramming/data/TEDS_2016.dta?raw=true](https://github.com/datageneration/home/blob/master/DataProgramming/data/TEDS_2016.dta?raw=true)")

# Assigning labels to the Tondu variable
TEDS_2016$Tondu <- factor(TEDS_2016$Tondu, 
                         levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9),
                         labels = c("Unification now", 
                                    "Status quo, unif. in future", 
                                    "Status quo, decide later", 
                                    "Status quo forever", 
                                    "Status quo, indep. in future", 
                                    "Independence now", 
                                    "No response"))
## 3. Exploratory Data Analysis
#| label: tondu-analysis
# Frequency Table
table(TEDS_2016$Tondu)

# Barchart
ggplot(TEDS_2016, aes(x = Tondu)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Tondu Variable",
       x = "Attitude",
       y = "Frequency") +
  coord_flip()
